steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts two recruitment methods: snowball sampling (through social networks) produced much higher retention than recruitment by strangers. The question asks what best explains why social-network recruitment led to higher retention.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘being recruited to participate in a study by someone with whom one is socially connected may impart a feeling of obligation to persist with participation in the study.’: directly explains the retention difference — a social bond creates pressure to continue participating. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘snowball sampling is more likely to improve retention rates among rural participants than among nonrural participants.’: addresses who is recruited, not why those recruits stayed longer. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘people with relatively small social networks are inherently less likely to be recruited to participate in a study via snowball sampling than are people with relatively large social networks.’: the passage does not compare motivation levels; this goes beyond what the data support. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘social networks can become large enough that two people can share a network but nevertheless regard each other as strangers.’: contradicts the passage, which treats social connection as the key variable. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
